'''Erel Kersos''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Doctor/Legends|surgeon]], holding the [[Military rank/Legends|rank]] of [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], and [[Commanding officer/Legends|Commanding Officer]] in charge of a [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|''MedStar''-class frigate]] during the [[Battle of Drongar]].

==Biography==
Coming from a very solidly [[Ensterite]] origins, Erel, from the [[Kersos]] clan, was a disciple of a long and traditional sociopolitical affiliation in which its core belief system was that no [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] could be made, much less consummated, outside the inhabitants of one’s own [[Star system/Legends|planetary system]].<ref name="MBS">''[[MedStar I: Battle Surgeons]]''</ref> In his youth, however, Erel defied this system by marring [[Feleema]], an outsider – an [[Ekster]], according to the ensterites traditionalists – giving up his clan name and renouncing everything, dealing with the terrible consequences of being forever shunned as a non-''permes'', a social and personal invisibility that did not diminished with [[Time/Legends|time]], or even with [[Death/Legends|death]].<ref name="MJH"/>

After six [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of marriage notwithstanding, Erel’s spouse Feleema [[Death/Legends|died]] in a [[Hovertrain/Legends|mag-lev]] accident on [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], alongside four hundred others. It was quick – a superconductor failed, the safeties malfunctioned, and the [[Train/Legends|train]] left the rail at three hundred [[Kilometer per hour/Legends|kilometers per hour]] and rammed into a row of deserted industrial buildings in the southern hemisphere. No survivors in any of the cars. Widowed and with no [[Youngling|children]], young Erel Kersos applied himself to his work and made a career in the [[Republic Military/Legends|military]], as a fresh [[Lieutenant/Legends|lieutenant]] in the service of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. His family and culture was no longer available to him: he wasn’t able to go home for his [[Parent/Legends|father]]’s [[Funeral/Legends|funeral]]; his [[Gender/Legends|mother]] considered him to be dead; plus, the nephews and nieces he never met, never saw, never even knew were [[Reproduction/Legends|born]].<ref name="MJH"/>

Nearly forty [[Galactic Standard Calendar|years]] later of having renounced his [[Ensterite]] heritage, at the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], Erel was promoted to [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] in the [[Republic Navy/Legends|Republic Navy]], and before applying to the duty of replacing [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Tarnese Bleyd]] after his [[Death/Legends|murder]] by an [[Assassin/Legends|assassin]] in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]], he found out his great-nephew, [[Jos Vondar]], was serving as [[Chief Surgeon]] at the [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 7]] on [[Drongar/Legends|Drongar]]. As the [[Commanding officer/Legends|Commanding Officer]] of the [[MedStar-class frigate/Legends|''MedStar''-class frigate]] in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]], Admiral Kersos inquired about his great-nephew and found out that Jos was having a romantic relationship with a [[Lorrdian/Legends|Lorrdian]] [[Nurse/Legends|nurse]], named [[Tolk le Trene]]. Determined to “spare him of grief” due to the trauma of his own personal experience, Erel intervened in the relationship by having a private conversation with Tolk on the matter, and explained the situation regarding his and Jos’ culture in such a way that it convinced her to request for a transfer to [[Republic Mobile Surgical Unit 3|Rimsoo 3]], across the [[Sea of Sponges]], a thousand [[Kilometer/Legends|klicks]] north away from Jos.<ref name="MJH"/>

Later on, after a moment of epiphany, [[Jos Vondar|Vondar]] realized how his great-uncle inconveniently intervened in his relationship with [[Tolk le Trene|Tolk]], and in a surge of ire went to see Admiral Kersos in his office abord the Medstar, questioning him for his attitude and demanding explanations. After an intense discussion in which the state of the ensterite culture and values were put to the test, with Jos even bringing up how the practice of [[Hustru fönster]] became more widespread in their [[Corellia/Legends|homeworld]], Erel, who saw his younger self in Jos in that moment by making a similar speech he once did to his brother, decided to support his great-nephew’s choice in any way he could. He corrected his mistake by having Tolk transferred back to Rimsoo 7, and even allowed her to watch the [[Recording rod|recording]] of the conversation he and Jos had as evidence.<ref name="MJH"/>

After the [[Battle of Drongar]], Admiral Erel Kersos requested to be reassigned to [[Borellos Base]] on Corellia, in order to accompany both Jos and Tolk and back them up with his support. Having spent practically his entire life alone and after finally finding some family, Erel felt ready to return home.<ref name="MJH"/>
